description: Generate platform icon files from SVG, update build_exe.py to use .ico, create Windows shortcut script, and update .desktop file for Linux icon integration
steps: Step 1: Create scripts/generate_icons.py that uses cairosvg and Pillow to convert src/stx/gui/assets/logo.svg into: (a) src/stx/gui/assets/logo.ico (multi-resolution: 16, 32, 48, 64, 128, 256px), (b) src/stx/gui/assets/logo.png (256x256 for Linux .desktop use). The script should be runnable standalone and should handle missing dependencies gracefully with a clear error message.
Step 2: Run generate_icons.py to produce the .ico and .png files, then commit them so they are always available without needing cairosvg at build time.

Step 3: Update build_exe.py to pass '--icon src/stx/gui/assets/logo.ico' to PyInstaller so the standalone Windows executable has the proper app icon. Also add the assets directory as --add-data so the icon is bundled inside the executable for runtime use.

Step 4: Create scripts/create_shortcut.py -- a Python script that creates a Windows desktop shortcut (.lnk file) using the win32com or winshell approach (with a fallback to PowerShell COM automation if pywin32 is not available). The shortcut should point to either the PyInstaller-built exe (if found in dist/) or the launch.bat, and use logo.ico as its icon. Include a --target option to choose exe vs bat.

Step 5: Update SalesforceTranslationHandler.desktop to set Icon= to the absolute path pattern using a relative path that XDG can resolve, or better, reference the logo.png. Add a comment explaining users should copy this file to ~/.local/share/applications/ and update the Icon= path to their actual install location. Also update Exec= to use the full path pattern.
Step 6: Add a brief section to README.md (or create INSTALL.md) documenting how to: (a) build the exe with icon, (b) create a Windows desktop shortcut, (c) install the Linux .desktop launcher.
Step 7: Run pytest tests/ to confirm nothing is broken by these additions.
acceptance_criteria: src/stx/gui/assets/logo.ico exists and contains multiple resolutions (16-256px)
build_exe.py includes --icon flag pointing to logo.ico
scripts/create_shortcut.py exists and can be run on Windows to create a .lnk shortcut
SalesforceTranslationHandler.desktop references logo.png as its Icon
pytest tests/ passes with no failures
verification: python scripts/generate_icons.py runs without error and produces .ico and .png files
python -c "from PIL import Image; img = Image.open('src/stx/gui/assets/logo.ico'); print(img.info)" confirms valid ICO
grep -q 'logo.ico' build_exe.py confirms icon is referenced
grep -q 'logo.png' SalesforceTranslationHandler.desktop confirms icon is referenced
findings: All 65 tests pass. Used python3 (not python) since pyenv does not have a bare python alias configured. cairosvg and Pillow installed successfully. The .ico contains all 6 resolutions (16-256px). The user wants a double-clickable desktop launcher experience similar to GitHub Desktop - this feature provides that via create_shortcut.py (Windows .lnk) and the .desktop file (Linux).
